message: Jake
Ash
Guy and Kristy have passed away, right?
Yeah.
Are you okay?
Yeah.
For now.
I hope that's the case.
Welcome.

In the sparsely populated bar, music is playing at a somewhat jarring volume, perhaps to avoid hearing the voices of the neighboring customers.
Does it seem like the whole bar is mourning the death of Guy, a major popular player, or is it just my imagination?
The bar has very few decorations. In such darkness, there probably aren’t many who want to strain their eyes to see those things.
He doesn’t look particularly depressed. Of course, he’s not an avid baseball fan, nor was he particularly close to Kristy.
He is staring at me intently, as if he thinks I might do something.
He is staring at me. I wonder why, even though he doesn’t seem to be probing, when looked at by him, I feel like revealing everything.
Somehow, unpleasant things just keep happening.
I can't help but feel like I'm the root of all evil.
What are you saying? That’s not true.
In times like this, you might not want to hear anything I say, but please, don't blame yourself.
Blame myself? I never intended to do that.
Then I won’t say anything.
Are you really okay?
You’re a more delicate and fragile person than you look. Please don’t think pessimistically.
There’s even a theory that it was suicide, but do you think Guy killed himself?
Isn't it a fact that the poor road conditions caused him to lose control of the steering?
If it was suicide, it could be my fault.
Again?
Thinking negatively won’t help, would it?
Listen, it was an accident. There’s no way Guy committed suicide over something you said.
That's just...
How do you know? Guy and Kristy might have died hating me.
That’s definitely not it. Making assumptions is not good.
Thinking everything is your responsibility is, in my opinion, arrogant. There’s only so much one person can do.
I was suspicious of Kristy; I thought that no matter how you look at it, she could be the culprit.
I was thinking something so terrible.
Maybe that was right, but maybe it wasn’t. I can’t say anything for sure at this point.
Even so, I had convinced myself that she was the perpetrator of the serial murder case, at least the events that took place in New York.
Blaming yourself like that won't bring her back.
That’s just...
I know that without being told.
Then please try to be more positive. Like deciding to catch the real culprit.
Something like that.
I just don't have what it takes to be a detective. It's impossible, I'm just an amateur.
You're quite the troubled person. It's fine to feel down, but try to hold yourself together.
There's no use thinking about it.
I think there are other things to consider.
The news suggested that Guy and the others are the culprits, but there's no evidence they did it.
That's right. Yet I ended up doubting them.
I doubted my friends. That's the worst.
Blaming yourself like that won't lead to anything.
At this point, we don't know who the culprit is, do we? Before blaming yourself, you should sort things out.
I had assumed Guy or Kristy was the culprit. Yet, those two ended up in that situation.
It's a case full of mysteries, but there must be clues somewhere. It's just that we aren't seeing them.
You need to calm down and reconsider the case from the beginning.
Even a pessimist like me can keep a cool head and observe the surroundings.
It's clear that someone like you is more suited to be a detective than I am.
You hadn't mentioned Guy or Kristy as suspect candidates before. If only I could have trusted those two.
It's about time.
I need to stop relying on him. I can't keep taking advantage of his kindness.
A driving mistake happened.
That sounds plausible, but I can't believe Guy would commit suicide. Everything should have been going well.
The best way to hide a murder is to make it look like an accident. It’s possible that Guy and the others were killed by someone.
If no more gruesome murders occur after this, it’ll be because Guy died, right? To frame Guy as the perpetrator.
She was finally at a point where she could be with the man she loved. This way, Kristy can't find peace.
Kristy is actually a serial killer, and Guy tried to commit suicide together with her when he found out.
Could that be a possibility?
I don’t feel good about speaking ill of the dead, but I can’t outright say that Kristy is innocent.
Now that Guy and Kristy, who were seen as suspects, are dead, the chances of another murder happening are probably low.
If murders stop here, it’ll mean that the dead Guy and Kristy were the culprits. It’s sad that we can’t refute this.
If it prevents more victims, I think that might be for the best.
Alright, let’s stay positive. Please think positively.
※※※ This is a bug ※※※
